consumes.artifact: specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml (when a prior spec exists, for completion gate check per fr-rf-012)
structured decision-log per fr-cc-if-008 (when brainstorming Q&A produced one; consumed for Phase 4 axis 2 check and Phase 6 mechanical authorization check per fr-rf-010)

produces.artifact: specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md (ephemeral handoff on R3 axis-1/2/3 block per fr-rf-015; consumed-and-deleted by brainstorming per fr-bs-008)

requirement.title: Three-Axis R3 Contradiction Check
requirement.description: Refiner MUST block when any of three contradiction axes fires, per Rule R3 (Block on Contradictions). The three axes: (1) design.md internal contradictions — including conflict between new design requirements and existing spec requirements when a prior spec exists, and broken dependencies on removed requirements; (2) design.md ↔ user Q&A — design says X and a structured decision-log row says ¬X; refiner has no authorization to silently pick either side; (3) spec-draft coverage — every produced criterion must trace to a (design phrase ∪ Q&A row) source; criteria with no such source are invention. Phasing: axes 1 and 2 are checked at Phase 4 (pre-draft, LLM judgment); axis 3 has LLM judgment at Phase 5.5 (per fr-rf-014) and mechanical enforcement at Phase 6 (per fr-rf-010). On any axis firing, refiner blocks: no spec.xml, no details/, no narrative report — the only persistent artifact written is the ephemeral handoff at specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md (per fr-rf-015), which is non-versioned and consumed-and-deleted by brainstorming per fr-bs-008.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a design doc where REQ-A requires "sessions expire after 15 minutes" and REQ-B requires "sessions never expire", refiner MUST block on axis 1 with the contradiction printed to terminal including specific requirement IDs and a pointer to specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md. Refiner MUST exit non-zero. 2026-04-27:A.2

Given a design phrase saying X and a structured decision-log row saying ¬X, refiner MUST block on axis 2. Silently picking either side is forbidden — even if the Q&A answer is more recent than the design. Terminal output MUST cite both the design line range and the Q&A q_id; the _pending-conflict.md handoff carries at least one candidate resolution per side ("keep design wording", "accept Q&A answer", "make the axis configurable so both stances coexist"). 2026-04-27:A.2

When blocked on any axis (1, 2, or 3), refiner MUST NOT write any authoritative artifact — no spec.xml, no file under specs/<spec-id>/details/, no refiner-report.md or any other persistent narrative reporting artifact. The only persistent artifact permitted on R3 axis-1/2/3 block is the ephemeral handoff specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md per fr-rf-015, which is non-versioned and consumed-and-deleted by brainstorming on successful new-design write per fr-bs-008. 2026-04-27:A.1

Terminal output on block MUST list each detected issue with: (i) which axis fired (1, 2, or 3) and a one-line description; (ii) the specific design line ranges and Q&A row q_ids involved. The 1–3 candidate resolutions and the user-action prompt directing the user to /arc-brainstorming iterate <spec-id> are written to _pending-conflict.md per fr-rf-015 — terminal output points the user to that file. 2026-04-27:A.2, 2026-04-27:B.2

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header
requirement.description: Every spec.xml MUST contain an identity header with spec_id, spec_version, status, source (design path + iteration date), scope, and supersedes fields.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A newly created spec.xml MUST have spec_version: 1, status: active, and supersedes empty or absent. REQ-F010
An updated spec.xml MUST have spec_version: base_version + 1, and supersedes set to "<spec-id>:v<previous-version>". REQ-F010

The source element MUST contain design_path pointing to the exact design doc file and design_iteration matching the design doc folder name. The folder name MUST start with an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) and MAY include a "-"-separated human-chosen suffix (e.g., "2026-04-16-v2" for same-date sequential iterations, "2026-04-16-oauth-pivot" for intent-tagged iterations). The suffix is a human convention, not a schema constraint. REQ-F010
The scope element MUST list included features with IDs and excluded items with reasons. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Per-Spec Directory Isolation
requirement.description: Multiple specs MUST coexist in separate specs/<spec-id>/ folders. Each folder is a self-contained project unit.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given spec-ids "auth" and "payments", their specs MUST reside at specs/auth/spec.xml and specs/payments/spec.xml respectively, with no cross-folder references. REQ-F011
Each spec folder MUST contain its own details/ subdirectory. Detail files MUST NOT reference requirements from other spec folders. REQ-F011

requirement.title: Formalize Spec From Design Doc
requirement.description: Refiner MUST read the complete design doc and produce or update a spec. There is no explicit mode distinction — the refiner checks whether specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists and adapts accordingly. When no existing spec is found, it formalizes from scratch. When an existing spec is found, it reads the design doc (Change Intent) alongside the existing spec, determines what changed, and updates the spec — like wiki ingest propagation.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When no existing spec.xml is found, refiner MUST produce a spec.xml with spec_version: 1 and detail files covering all requirements identified in the design doc, each with at least one acceptance criterion and trace. REQ-F012

When an existing spec.xml is found, refiner MUST read both the design doc and the existing spec. ADDED items result in new requirements; MODIFIED items update existing requirements in place; REMOVED items delete requirements from detail files. REQ-F013
When updating an existing spec, requirements NOT affected by the design doc's change intent MUST remain unchanged in the output spec. REQ-F013

A complete rewrite (aggressive iteration where the Change Intent replaces everything) MUST be handled by the same behavior — the refiner does not switch to a "replace" code path. The design doc's Change Intent drives what gets added/modified/removed/renamed; the refiner emits a correspondingly large <delta>. No mode labels, no separate behavior — just one refiner that produces whatever delta the situation requires. REQ-F012, REQ-F013

requirement.title: Delta Metadata Output — Wiki-Style Accumulation
requirement.description: When updating an existing spec, refiner MUST append a new <delta> element to the spec's <overview> recording what changed in this version. Refiner MUST NOT overwrite, modify, or remove prior <delta> elements — they are historical record. <overview> accumulates all <delta> children ordered ascending by version, producing a self-contained iteration history inside the spec (wiki-style, per Karpathy three-layer model). This metadata stays within the spec (wiki layer) so the planner can determine planning scope without reading the design doc (raw source layer).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A new <delta> element MUST include: version (matching the new spec_version), iteration (matching the new design_iteration), and child elements for each change. Supported child elements are <added ref="req-id" />, <modified ref="req-id" />, <removed ref="req-id"><reason>...</reason></removed> (reason required; optional <migration> child), and <renamed ref_old="..." ref_new="..."> (optional <reason>; body unchanged — semantic changes use removed+added, not renamed). REQ-F013
For a new spec (no prior version), no delta element is written. Absence of any <delta> in <overview> signals "plan all requirements" to the planner. REQ-F012

Every requirement ID referenced in the new delta MUST correspond to an actual requirement in the spec's detail files (for added/modified/ renamed.ref_new) or to a requirement that existed in a prior version (for removed.ref and renamed.ref_old). REQ-F013

Refiner MUST preserve all prior <delta> elements verbatim when writing the new spec. The new <delta> is appended as the last child of <overview>. The resulting sequence MUST be ordered ascending by version. Refiner MUST NOT merge, deduplicate, or rewrite historical deltas. REQ-F013
The new delta's version attribute MUST equal the new spec_version. The new delta's iteration attribute MUST equal the new design_iteration. Mismatch is ERROR. REQ-F013

requirement.title: DAG Completion Gate (Upstream of New Iteration)
requirement.description: Before producing a new spec version (one that would append a <delta> and increment spec_version), refiner MUST check the current specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml for sprint completion. This gate lives in refiner, not planner, so that inconsistent wiki-layer state ("spec at v(N+1) while prior sprint's DAG is still incomplete") never arises. If the gate blocks, nothing downstream moves — spec stays at v(N), DAG stays at v(N)'s execution state, user completes the work or abandons the entire spec, then the refiner may run cleanly.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When no prior spec.xml exists, refiner MUST NOT perform the gate check (there is no prior sprint to be incomplete). Refiner proceeds as v1 formalization. REQ-F023
When a prior spec.xml exists AND no dag.yaml exists at specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml, refiner MUST proceed. This is a legal state (user refined without planning yet). REQ-F023
When a prior spec.xml exists AND dag.yaml exists AND all epics in dag.yaml are in "completed" status, refiner MUST proceed with the new iteration. REQ-F023

When a prior spec.xml exists AND dag.yaml exists AND any epic is NOT in "completed" status, refiner MUST BLOCK. It MUST print to terminal: the number of incomplete epics, the specific epic IDs and their statuses, and the message "Complete current sprint before iterating." Refiner MUST exit non-zero. No spec file is written, no <delta> is added, nothing changes on disk. No refiner-report.md is written. REQ-F023

Refiner MUST NOT implement any escape hatch for the gate — no --force flag, no environment variable override, no "abandoned" epic status that bypasses the check. The only legitimate paths forward when blocked are: (a) the user completes the remaining epics, or (b) the user abandons the entire spec by deleting specs/<spec-id>/ (which is a filesystem action outside refiner's scope). Partial abandonment is rejected by design because it would pollute dag.yaml status semantics. REQ-F023

requirement.title: Spec Version Increment
requirement.description: When updating an existing spec, refiner MUST increment the spec version and record supersedes. The planner uses spec_version to locate the correct delta for planning scope.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Updated spec.xml MUST have spec_version = previous version + 1. REQ-F010
Updated spec.xml MUST set supersedes to "<spec-id>:v<previous-version>". REQ-F010
Updated spec.xml source MUST point to the new design doc iteration that triggered this update. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Merged Spec Validation
requirement.description: After producing a new or updated spec, refiner MUST validate that the resulting spec has no internal contradictions before writing output. This requirement covers inter-requirement merge contradictions — contradictions across requirements introduced by merging the design's Change Intent with the prior spec. Intra-requirement self-contradictions (description ↔ AC scope mismatches, RFC-2119 verb mismatches inside a single requirement) are covered separately by fr-rf-014's Phase 5.5 sub-pass.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given an update that adds a requirement contradicting an existing requirement, refiner MUST block per fr-rf-001 (terminal output, non-zero exit, no files written). The contradiction MUST be described in terminal output with specific requirement IDs. REQ-F015
Given an update that modifies a requirement into contradiction with another requirement, refiner MUST block. REQ-F015
Given an update that removes a requirement depended on by another requirement still in the spec, refiner MUST block with a "dependency broken" issue. REQ-F015

requirement.title: Input Validation — Design Doc
requirement.description: Before any formalization, refiner MUST validate its input per the design doc contract (fr-cc-if-001). All validation checks run before any output is produced. The refiner consumes the complete design doc.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Refiner MUST verify the design doc file exists at the provided path. Missing file is ERROR with remediation: "Run brainstorming to produce a design doc first." REQ-F008

Refiner MUST check filesystem state to decide whether version-dependent fields are produced: if specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists → read existing spec + design doc, produce updated spec with incremented spec_version, supersedes, and a new <delta>. If not → read design doc, produce new spec with spec_version=1, no supersedes, no delta. This is a single behavior with conditional fields, not two modes. REQ-F012, REQ-F013

When a prior spec exists, refiner MUST validate that the design doc has the required sections for an iteration design doc: Context section (with spec version reference) and Change Intent section present. Missing required sections is ERROR with remediation: "When a prior spec exists, the design doc must include Context and Change Intent sections — re-run brainstorming with the prior spec in scope." REQ-F005, REQ-F008

When a prior spec exists, if the design doc's date folder is older than or equal to the spec's recorded design_iteration, refiner MUST produce WARNING: "design iteration <date> is not newer than spec source <spec-date> — this may be a stale design doc." REQ-F026

requirement.title: Output Validation — Two-Pass Write with Mechanical Authorization Check
requirement.description: Refiner MUST follow the two-pass write pattern (fr-cc-val-001): build the complete spec.xml and details/ in memory, validate against the Spec Identity Header contract (fr-cc-if-002) AND perform the mechanical authorization check (axis 3 enforcement at validator level), then write to disk only if all validation passes. The mechanical authorization check iterates over every <trace> element in the in-memory spec and verifies the cited content appears at the cited source location: for traces citing design line ranges, the cited content MUST appear at those lines; for traces citing Q&A rows by q_id, the cited content MUST appear in that row's user_answer_verbatim of the structured decision-log. This is the validator-level layer of axis 3 enforcement; the LLM-judgment layer fires earlier at Phase 5.5 per fr-rf-014.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After building spec.xml in memory, refiner MUST validate the identity header per fr-cc-if-002: spec_id, spec_version, status, source, scope all present and well-formed. Missing fields are ERROR. REQ-F010, REQ-F015
Every <requirement> in the built output MUST have at least one <acceptance_criteria> with a <trace> element. Requirements without testable AC are ERROR. REQ-F012

If output validation finds ERRORs, refiner MUST report all findings with remediation guidance and MUST NOT write any authoritative files to specs/<spec-id>/ (no spec.xml, no details/). The ephemeral _pending-conflict.md handoff per fr-rf-015 is the carved-out exception on R3 axis-1/2/3 blocks per ac5; for non-R3-axis ERRORs (e.g. identity-header errors per ac1/ac2, or schema-validation errors per fr-rf-010-ac1/ac2/ac4) no files MUST be written at all. REQ-F008, REQ-F015, 2026-04-27:A.1

If output validation passes (zero ERRORs), refiner MUST write all files atomically — spec.xml and all details/*.xml in a single operation. Partial writes (spec.xml written but details/ incomplete) MUST NOT occur. REQ-F012

Refiner MUST perform the mechanical authorization check at Phase 6. For every <trace> element in the in-memory draft: (i) if the trace cites a design line range, refiner MUST verify the cited content appears at those lines in the design doc; (ii) if the trace cites a Q&A row by q_id, refiner MUST verify the cited content appears in that row's user_answer_verbatim of the structured decision-log per fr-cc-if-008. Any mismatch is ERROR. On axis-3 mechanical-check ERROR, refiner MUST block per fr-rf-001 axis 3 — the _pending-conflict.md handoff per fr-rf-015 is written, no spec.xml or details/ are written. 2026-04-27:B.4

requirement.title: Phase 5 No-Invention Discipline
requirement.description: During Phase 5 (drafting the spec in memory), refiner MUST NOT author any criterion from training-data inference. When a design phrase is qualitative ("rate-limited", "fast", "secure") or a Q&A row defers ("use defaults", "covered.", "skip", "you decide", or similar deferral phrases with deferral_signal=true per fr-cc-if-008), the legitimate refiner moves are exactly three: (a) preserve the design's qualitative phrasing as SHOULD/MAY (the source phrase is in design.md — that is the authorization); (b) leave the axis unbound (no criterion at all on that axis; downstream stages may surface it as a planning question); (c) BLOCK on axis 3 with a candidate-resolution handoff via fr-rf-015. Inventing a concrete MUST from training-data common practice is not among the legitimate moves.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a design phrase "the system is rate-limited" with no concrete rate value, and no decision-log row resolving the rate, refiner MUST NOT author "rate limit MUST be 60 requests per minute" (or any other concrete value) — that would be invention. Legitimate moves: (a) emit "the system SHOULD be rate-limited" with trace to the qualitative design phrase; (b) leave the rate axis unbound (no criterion); (c) BLOCK on axis 3. 2026-04-27:A.3

Given a Q&A row whose user_answer_verbatim matches a deferral signal (deferral_signal=true), refiner MUST treat the corresponding axis as unbound — deferral does NOT authorize a concrete MUST derived from training-data common practice. The three legitimate moves still apply. 2026-04-27:A.3

For every concrete MUST in the produced spec, refiner MUST be able to point to a non-deferral source — either a design phrase containing the concrete value, or a Q&A row whose user_answer_verbatim contains the concrete value with deferral_signal=false. If no such source exists, the criterion is invention and MUST NOT be authored (use one of the three legitimate moves instead). 2026-04-27:A.3

requirement.title: Phase 5.5 — Self-Contradiction Sub-Pass and Axis 3 LLM Judgment
requirement.description: Before Phase 6 output validation, refiner MUST perform a Phase 5.5 LLM sub-pass that hosts two checks: (a) self-contradiction — re-read each requirement's <description> against each <criterion> (and against sibling criteria) to detect scope mismatches (description says "the system handles X" but ACs only test the success path) and RFC-2119 verb mismatches (description uses MUST but a sibling AC uses SHOULD on the same axis); (b) axis 3 LLM judgment — for each criterion in the in-memory draft, verify a (design phrase ∪ Q&A row) source can be cited; criteria with no citable source are invention per fr-rf-013. Both findings are R3 enforcement failures (BLOCK + non-zero exit + no spec.xml + no details/), with severity equal to Patterns 1a/1b/2 — not WARN. Phase 5.5 findings are not silently corrected; they BLOCK and route to recovery. Axis-3 LLM-coverage findings trigger the _pending-conflict.md handoff per fr-rf-015-ac1 (axis 3 enumerated). Self-contradiction findings (description ↔ AC scope mismatch and RFC-2119 verb mismatch per ac1/ac2) ALSO trigger the handoff so the user has a single one-message recovery path covering every R3 BLOCK surface; see ac5 for the contract.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a requirement whose description says "the system handles X under both success and failure paths" but whose ACs only test the success path, refiner MUST detect the scope mismatch at Phase 5.5 and BLOCK with terminal output including the requirement ID and a remediation hint ("widen ACs to cover failure path, or narrow description to match ACs"). 2026-04-27:A.4

Given a requirement whose description uses "MUST validate" but whose AC for the same axis uses "SHOULD validate" (or vice versa), refiner MUST detect the RFC-2119 verb mismatch at Phase 5.5 and BLOCK with terminal output including the requirement ID and a remediation hint ("align verbs across description and ACs for the same axis"). 2026-04-27:A.4

Phase 5.5 MUST also perform axis 3 coverage LLM judgment: for every criterion in the in-memory draft, refiner verifies a citable source (design phrase or non-deferral Q&A row) exists. Criteria with no citable source trigger BLOCK per fr-rf-001 axis 3. This is the LLM-judgment layer; the mechanical layer fires at Phase 6 per fr-rf-010-ac5. 2026-04-27:A.2, 2026-04-27:A.4

On any Phase 5.5 finding (self-contradiction per ac1/ac2 OR axis-3 LLM coverage per ac3), refiner MUST treat it as R3 enforcement severity — exit non-zero, no spec.xml, no details/, no narrative report. Phase 5.5 findings MUST NOT be downgraded to WARNING. A WARN would let the spec ship with internal contradictions, which is precisely Pattern 3 of the eval evidence. 2026-04-27:A.4

On any Phase 5.5 finding (self-contradiction per ac1/ac2 OR axis-3 LLM coverage per ac3), refiner MUST also write the _pending-conflict.md handoff per fr-rf-015's contract. This is the single recovery surface for every R3 BLOCK — self-contradiction is not exempted. The seed conflict_description carries the requirement ID and the relevant remediation hint from ac1/ac2/ac3. 2026-04-27:A.4, 2026-04-27:B.2

requirement.title: _pending-conflict.md Write-on-Block Contract
requirement.description: When refiner blocks on axis 1, 2, or 3 (per fr-rf-001), it MUST write a single structured handoff file at specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md and exit non-zero. Per A.1's narrowed Iron Law clause 3 (NEVER WRITE AUTHORITATIVE STATE ON BLOCK), this file is the explicit ephemeral exception: non-versioned, non-authoritative, and consumed-and-deleted by brainstorming on successful new-design write per fr-bs-008. The file's purpose is to enable arc-brainstorming Phase 0 iterate-branch auto-entry without requiring the user to retell the conflict.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: On axis-1, axis-2, or axis-3 block (per fr-rf-001 or per fr-rf-010-ac5 for axis-3 mechanical), refiner MUST write specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md with content per fr-cc-if-007: which axis fired (1, 2, or 3), the conflict description (specific design line ranges and Q&A row q_ids involved), 1–3 candidate resolutions, and a user-action prompt directing the user to /arc-brainstorming iterate <spec-id>. 2026-04-27:B.2

Refiner MUST NOT write _pending-conflict.md for non-R3-axis blocks: DAG completion gate failure per fr-rf-012, design-doc validation failure per fr-rf-009, or identity-header validation errors per fr-rf-010-ac1 through fr-rf-010-ac4. These are pipeline-mechanical or programmer-error blocks, not axis contradictions; their output channels are terminal and exit code only. 2026-04-27:B.2
